generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components
instance LightPowerSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor representing Revit light power value in watts extracted from enriched BIM metadata."
    DTDFVocab:producedData LightPowerData
]

instance LuxSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor representing photometric lux value extracted from BIM."
    DTDFVocab:producedData LuxData
]

instance KelvinSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor representing photometric color temperature in Kelvin extracted from BIM."
    DTDFVocab:producedData KelvinData
]

instance TemperatureProbeSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Temperature probe sensor modeled in BIM, measuring indoor air temperature."
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureData
]

instance HumidityProbeSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Humidity probe sensor modeled in BIM, measuring indoor relative humidity."
    DTDFVocab:producedData HumidityData
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance LightPowerData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LightPowerSensor
]

instance LuxData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LuxSensor
]

instance KelvinData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom KelvinSensor
]

instance TemperatureData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureProbeSensor
]

instance HumidityData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HumidityProbeSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance LightPowerDataValue : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Persisted light power value received through Ditto, used for visual rendering."
    DTDFVocab:fromData LightPowerData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseDittoEnabler
]

instance LuxDataValue : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Persisted lux value received through Ditto, used for visual rendering."
    DTDFVocab:fromData LuxData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo UE5VisualizerEnabler
]

instance KelvinDataValue : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Persisted Kelvin value received through Ditto, used for visual rendering."
    DTDFVocab:fromData KelvinData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo UE5VisualizerEnabler
]

instance TemperatureDataValue : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Persisted temperature value received through Ditto, used for HVAC simulation."
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseDittoEnabler
]

instance HumidityDataValue : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Persisted humidity value received through Ditto, used for HVAC simulation."
    DTDFVocab:fromData HumidityData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseDittoEnabler
]

instance EnrichedBIMModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Enriched BIM model including semantic artifacts of sensors and actuators extracted via MDE."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseDittoEnabler, EclipseHonoEnabler
]

instance PhotometricMetadataModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Model representing photometric metadata (watts, lux, Kelvin) parsed from Revit for visualization."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo UE5VisualizerEnabler
]

instance ThermalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Thermal behavior model derived from BIM for HVAC simulation."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseDittoEnabler
]

instance HVACControlModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Control logic model for HVAC system extracted from BIM."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseDittoEnabler
]

instance LightingControlModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Control logic model for lighting system extracted from BIM."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo UE5VisualizerEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance EclipseHonoEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enabler providing ingestion of sensor telemetry via Eclipse Hono."
    DTDFVocab:enables OptimizationAnalyticsService
]

instance EclipseDittoEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enabler synchronizing device states with Eclipse Ditto."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, ControlInterfaceService
]

instance UE5VisualizerEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enabler rendering visualizations in Unreal Engine 5 using photometric metadata."
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationDashboardService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service offering real‐time monitoring of building states derived from integrated sensor/actuator models."
    DTDFVocab:provides MonitoringInsight
]

instance OptimizationAnalyticsService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing optimisation and predictive analytics on building operation."
    DTDFVocab:provides OptimizationInsight
]

instance ControlInterfaceService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service exposing control logic to Facility Managers for interactive operations."
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlAction
]

instance VisualizationDashboardService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service delivering immersive dashboards for inspection and decision support."
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualizationInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance MonitoringInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight providing live status of building subsystems."
]

instance OptimizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight offering predictive optimisation recommendations."
]

instance ControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action enabling Facility Manager to modify control set‑points remotely."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance VisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight presenting real‑time visual representation of building environment."
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance LightFixtureActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Actuator representing a lighting fixture instantiated in BIM and exposed as a TwinComponent in UE5, linked to physical hardware through DittoController."
]

instance HvacDamperActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Actuator representing an HVAC damper instantiated in BIM, exposed as a TwinComponent in UE5, controlled via DittoController and Hono."
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "Control flows from the digital twin to the physical building are facilitated by the same bidirectional data exchange mechanism described in the framework. The documents note that the DT framework acts as a central hub enabling real‑time monitoring and control via facilities managers, but do not detail the specific command types, control message formats, validation, authentication, feedback loops, conflict resolution, or confirmation of command execution. The focus remains on providing an interface for real‑time monitoring and control."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The framework supports continuous, live bidirectional synchronization between the physical building and its digital counterpart. Real‑time data exchange ensures that state changes in the physical system are reflected in the virtual twin with negligible latency. The document does not provide explicit polling intervals, latency limits, or temporal granularity specifications."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DT constellation follows a modular, extensible pattern. Building Control Engineers create device models in BIM tools and deploy them to the DT Framework. The framework offers standardized extension points, enabling DT Engineers to plug in custom analytics and services. Runtime configuration management allows adjustments without full redeployment. The central hub manages resources, ensures scalability, and maintains system‑wide coherence across the building life cycle."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "Phase 1 – Device Modeling: Building Control Engineers enrich BIM models with explicit sensor, actuator, and controller elements, creating a unified semantic foundation.
Phase 2 – Model Transformation: An MDE (Model-Driven Engineering) pipeline automatically extracts structured representations from the enriched BIM models.
Phase 3 – Artifact Generation: The system generates deployable Digital Twin artifacts (e.g., micro‑service bundles or container images) with minimal manual intervention.
Phase 4 – Deployment: Building Control Engineers initiate real‑time DT deployment directly from BIM, provisioning the required controllers and data pipelines.
Phase 5 – Operation: Facility Managers continuously monitor system states, interact with the twin, and control building operations via the twin’s real‑time data exchange layer.
Phase 6 – Evolution: DT Engineers extend functionality through custom services and plugins, leveraging standardized extension points in the modular architecture.
Governance: Actor roles defined in Table I (Control Engineer, Facility Manager, DT Engineer) coordinate responsibility; lifecycle transitions are automatable; a modular architecture ensures version consistency and rollback capability."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Model fidelity is guaranteed through the MDE transformation’s validation step, where the enriched BIM is checked against the IFC schema and custom rules; runtime data integrity is enforced by Ditto’s policy‑based validation (ensuring sensor readings fall within defined ranges and units) and by Hono’s at‑least‑once message delivery coupled with Ditto’s persistent event storage, which together provide fault tolerance and audit trails for data consistency."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The technical networking of the DT framework is described primarily in terms of interoperability across heterogeneous systems and scalability from components to entire infrastructure, but specific communication protocols, network topology, bandwidth, latency, reliability, redundancy, or security protocols are not explicitly defined in the documents. The framework’s modular architecture and standardized extension points imply support for modern connectivity standards, but exact technical specifications are not provided."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "Deployment details for the DT framework are discussed in terms of runtime configuration management, allowing adjustments without full redeployment, and modular architecture for evolvability. The documents do not specify whether the DT is hosted on cloud, edge, or on‑premise platforms, nor do they provide computational and storage sizing, scalability mechanisms, deployment models, or availability and reliability assurance strategies. The framework is presented as a central hub facilitating bidirectional data exchange for building lifecycle management, but hosting infrastructure specifics are omitted."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "Horizontal integration is facilitated through the framework’s standardized extension points and bidirectional data exchange interfaces, enabling seamless interoperability with heterogeneous enterprise systems and legacy infrastructures. External systems and other Digital Twins can connect via these interfaces, exchange sensor data, and issue control commands, thereby preserving interoperability and low‑overhead communication across the building ecosystem."
]
